LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: THE SM COMPANIES, LLC FKA THE SAVE MART COMPANIES, INC.'S SECOND NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT WITH PREJUDICE. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT THE SM COMPANIES, LLC FKA THE SAVE MART COMPANIES, INC.'S SECOND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT WITH PREJUDICE, WHICH IS UNOPPOSED, IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. ***SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING*** JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ) ***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS SIGNED.

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-20-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ALI SADIQ, AND THE SM COMPANIES LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE COLANGELO DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF PROVIDED LEGAL SUPPORT FOR HIS POSITIONS DURING THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.41(A)(1). THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE JULY 18, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Continued To Mar-12-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Second Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: The SM Companies, LLC fka The Save Mart Companies, Inc.'s Second Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Specially Appearing Defendant The SM Companies, LLC fka The Save Mart Companies, Inc.'s second motion to quash service of summons and motion to dismiss complaint with prejudice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The motion to quash service is GRANTED. The motion to dismiss with prejudice is DENIED. The latter motion is made under California Rules of Court, rule 3.110. Under that rule, a failure to timely serve a complaint are addressed by way of an order to show cause regarding sanctions. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 3.110(f).) In this court, such orders to show cause are not issued or addressed by this department. Nor is dismissal appropriate under Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10, subd. (a)(3). This section authorizes motions to dismiss "pursuant to the applicable provisions of Chapter 1.5 (commencing with Section 583.110) of Title 8." Subdivision (a) of section 583.210 provides, "The summons and complaint shall be served upon a defendant within three years after the action is commenced against the defendant." Three years have not yet passed. The court recognizes the motion is unopposed, but it fails on the law and, therefore, is denied. Of course, the parties remain free to stipulate to a dismissal with prejudice and file the appropriate documents to achieve that result.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, an order is signed.
